description_long: This object is a classic stick grenade, commonly known as a 'potato masher' due to its distinctive shape. The head is a cylindrical metal canister painted olive green, featuring white stenciled German text and a prominent metal clip on the side. The handle is made of wood, showing a natural grain and some wear, and is attached to the metal head with a reinforced collar. The bottom of the handle has a threaded metal cap, which is typically unscrewed to access the fuse mechanism. The overall construction combines metal and wood, giving it a rugged, utilitarian appearance. The object is compact and designed to be easily gripped and thrown by hand.
description: A German stick grenade with a green metal canister head, wooden handle, and white stenciled text, featuring a metal clip and threaded cap.
description_view_0: The grenade is shown in full profile, highlighting the green canister head with white text, the metal clip, and the wooden handle with visible grain.
description_view_1: This angled view emphasizes the length of the wooden handle and the cylindrical shape of the metal head, with the clip and text partially visible.
description_view_2: The grenade is seen from a slightly elevated angle, showing the top of the canister, the handle, and the attachment collar in detail.
description_view_3: A top-down view focusing on the circular end of the canister, with the clip and some camouflage wear visible on the head.
attribute_detonation_mechanism: The grenade is likely detonated by unscrewing the metal cap at the base of the handle to access and pull a friction igniter, which triggers the internal fuse.
